---
title: "p-demotion-discourse"
author: "Cristian R. Juárez"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: 
    latex_engine: xelatex
    keep_tex: true
    number_sections: true
geometry: margin=1in
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{fontspec}
  - \setmainfont{Libertinus Serif}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)
library(viridisLite)
```

-   Load data and first exploration

```{r}
mocCA190924 <- read.delim("/Users/cristian/Documents/postdocs/research/abst_pprs_docmnt/2025-pdemot-moc/raw_data/mocCA190924_text.tsv", sep = "\t", header = T)
# glimpse(mocCA190924)
# head(mocCA190924)
```

-   Distribution of predicate types (ok)

```{r message=TRUE, warning=TRUE, paged.print=TRUE}
mocCA190924 |> 
  filter(!is.na(val_type), val_type != "", val_type != "avalent" ) |>
  arrange(val_type) |> 
   count(val_type) |> 
    arrange(desc(n)) |>
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) |>
  arrange(proportion) |>
ggplot(aes(x = reorder( val_type, proportion), y = proportion, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
# Add counts and proportion in bars  
geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "(", scales::percent(proportion), ")")), 
            vjust = -0.4,
            size  = 2.3,
            color = "black") +
   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  
# If you prefer percentage ticks, use this instead:
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5), labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5)) +
  #scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray90", high = "gray10") +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Predicate type",
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme( 
# Panel (plotting area) background
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#F2F2F2", colour = NA),
    # Add a subtle border around the panel (optional)
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "#BDBDBD", size = 0.5),
  panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
# Outer background (outside plotting area)
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 9, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 9, r = 9, b = 0, l = 0)), 
)  # Tilt labels 45 degrees)

# Save plot in project's folder
ggsave(filename = "/Users/cristian/Documents/postdocs/research/abst_pprs_docmnt/2025-pdemot-moc/figures/proportion-predicates.png",
  plot = last_plot(),
  width = 7, height = 4,
  dpi = 600,          # minimum for print
)

```

-   Correspondence of predicates in -ɢan or -taɢan alternations. (ok)

```{r}
 # Table: distribution of predicates in text
 predicates <- mocCA190924 |> 
   rename(predicate_type = val_type) |>
   filter(!is.na(verb_in_sentence), verb_in_sentence != "", str_detect(predicate_type, "-ɢan|-taɢan")) |>  # simplified search with regular expression. Better option
   count(verb_in_sentence, predicate_type) |> 
   arrange(desc(n))
 predicates
 
 # Create table for document
library(xtable) 
predicates_derived <- xtable(predicates,
             caption = "Predicates by valency subtype",
             label   = "tab:predicates")
print(predicates_derived)
```

-   Observe correspondences between expression/abscence of P arguments (ok)

```{r}
# Table: val_type vs P_arg
p_expression <- mocCA190924 |> 
   rename(predicate_type = val_type) |> 
  filter(!is.na(predicate_type), predicate_type != "", str_detect(predicate_type, "-ɢan|-taɢan"),
         !is.na(P_arg), P_arg != "", P_arg != "not_applicable") |> 
  count(predicate_type, P_arg) |> 
  arrange(desc(n)) |>
 mutate( P_arg = recode(P_arg,
      "yes"            = "P_present",
     "no"             = "P_absent")) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = P_arg, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

# Check output 
 p_expression

# Create table for document
library(xtable) 
p_expression_tab <- xtable(p_expression,
             caption = "P expression",
             label   = "tab:p_expression")
print(p_expression_tab)
```

-   Additional data organization: Test for semantic map (not implemented in the paper)

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
# Optional plotting libs used later:
# library(ggplot2); library(viridis); library(ggrepel)

# Start from your data frame: mocCA190924
df_sem <- mocCA190924 %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(verb_in_sentence), verb_in_sentence != "",
    !is.na(val_type), val_type != "", val_type != "not_applicable",
    val_type != "avalent"                       # you asked to drop this earlier
  )

# Optional: focus on P-demotion-relevant tokens
# df_sem <- df_sem %>% filter(P_arg == "yes")   # uncomment if you want this restriction

# Optional: keep only valency types that occur >= 2 tokens (stability)
min_n <- 1
keep_val <- df_sem %>% count(val_type) %>% filter(n >= min_n) %>% pull(val_type)
df_sem <- df_sem %>% filter(val_type %in% keep_val)

library(tidyr)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

# 1) Build contingency table: predicates × valency types
tab <- df_sem %>%
  count(verb_in_sentence, val_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = val_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("verb_in_sentence")
tab

# 2) Run CA
res.ca <- CA(tab, graph = FALSE)
# 3) Plot a clean biplot (semantic map)
fviz_ca_biplot(
  res.ca,
  repel = TRUE,
  col.row = "#2b2b2b",   # predicates
  col.col = "#8c2d04",   # valency types
  label = "all",
  title = "Predicate × Valency CA biplot (Mocoví)"
)
```
